content.full_text:
City of
Encinitas

June 8, 1992

Mr. Scott Halibert
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Sir:

Deborah Cervone, Deputy City Clerk, sent a letter dated May 22,
1992, regarding my ability to discuss an issue currently before the
City Council.     (see attached).

It now appears that the traffic study area will be expanded to
include the street in which my home is located.     Any change in
traffic circulation would not affect the traffic on my street in
any way as it is a residential street (see map).  Please advise me
if, in your opinion, I am able to participate in the decision on
this issue.

Sincerely,

John Davis
Deputy Mayor

527 Encinitas Boulevard, Encinitas, California 92024     TEL 619-944-5050 / FAX 619-632-9836     recycled paper

City of
Encinitas

May 22, 1992

Maura Wiegand
Mayor

Mr. Scott Halibert
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

John Daris
Deputy Mayor

Dear Sir:

I spoke to Mr. John Wallace today and he suggested that
I write for a legal opinion as to our Deputy Mayor's
ability to discuss and take action on the following
issue:

Anne Omsted
Council Member

There currently exists a traffic barrier located at the
end of Crest Drive; a residential road in the City of
Encinitas. Our City Council is considering removing that
barrier. Deputy Mayor Davis's home is located on Lahoud
Drive, 1 1/4 mile from the barrier. Lake Drive is a
circulation element road and 100 feet from his home. (see
map)

Pamela Slater
Council Member

Our Council will be discussing this issue on June 10th
and would greatly appreciate a response prior to that
date.

Thank you in advance for any assistance you can provide.

E. Gail Hano
Council Member

Sincerely,

Warren H. Shafer
City Manager

Deborah Cervone
Deputy City Clerk

James F. Benson
Assistant City Manager

527 Encinitas Boulevard, Encinitas, California 92024

TEL 619-944-5050 / FAX 619-632-9836

recycled paper

EAST CARDIFF
TRAFFIC DIVERSION STUDY

STUDY AREA

STREETS TO BE EVALUATED

KEY INTERSECTIONS TO BE
ANALYZED

SANTA FE DR.

WOODLAKE DR.

WOODGROVE DR.

BIRMINGHAM DR.

EL CAMINO REAL CRESCENT DR.

CHESTER DR.

MANCHESTER

SAN
DIEGO

MANCHESTER AVE

EL CAMINO REAL

127

5

[illegible]

(see attached). It now appears that the traffic study area will be expanded to include the street in which my home is located. Any change in traffic circulation would not affect the traffic on my street in any way as it is a residential street (see map). Please advise me if, in your opinion, I am able to participate in the decision on this issue. Sincerely, John Davis Deputy Mayor City of Encinitas May 22, 1992 Mr. Scott Halibert General Counsel Fair Political Practices Commission P.O. Box 807 Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 Dear Sir: I spoke to Mr. John Wallace today and he suggested that I write for a legal opinion as to our Deputy Mayor's ability to discuss and take action on the following issue: There currently exists a traffic barrier located at the end of Crest Drive; a residential road in the City of Encinitas. Our City Council is considering removing that barrier. Deputy Mayor Davis's home is located on Lahoud Drive, 1 1/4 mile from the barrier. Lake Drive is a circulation element road and 100 feet from his home. (see map) Our Council will be discussing this issue on June 10th and would greatly appreciate a response prior to that date. Thank you in advance for any assistance you can provide. Sincerely, Deborah Cervone Deputy City Clerk This map is titled "East Cardiff Traffic Diversion Study." It shows the study area, streets to be evaluated, and key intersections to be analyzed. The map includes several streets such as Santa Fe Dr, Windsor Rd, Woodlake Dr, Crest Dr, Lake Dr, Birmingham Dr, El Camino Real, Manchester Ave, San Diego, and others. There are also some labeled intersections and roads, but the majority of the street names are not fully visible or legible in this image. The map is marked with a legend indicating: - Study Area (shaded) - Streets to Be Evaluated (solid black line) - Key Intersections to Be Analyzed (circles) The map appears to be a technical or planning document, likely used for urban planning or traffic management purposes. The image you provided is completely white, with no visible content or text. If you have another page or section of the document that you would like to transcribe or discuss, please provide it.
